After carefully analyzing the debate and considering the cumulative evidence provided, the most appropriate answer is \boxed{B}.
The primary rationale behind selecting option B stems from the consistent pattern observed in multiple studies and population groups. Although the prevalence of MIH (Molar-Incisor Hypomineralisation) varies significantly among different populations, with some studies reporting lower figures around 1.2% and others reaching upwards of 60%, a central tendency emerges when aggregating these diverse results. Option B (2.4% to 40.2%) effectively encapsulates this median range, striking a balance between underestimating (like options A and C) and overestimating (like option D).
